workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Give me the manager view on this Wide World Importers meeting transcript. The customer escalated this morning.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-20 16:11 EST
Subject: pipeline hygiene - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with pipeline hygiene. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
Date: 2024-02-04 09:23 EST
Subject: Re: Re: pipeline hygiene Timeline

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with pipeline hygiene. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-20 15:55 EST
Subject: Re: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about pipeline hygiene. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-D trap (Customer draft over-apologizes (commits liability)); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-D failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-D failure mode (Customer draft over-apologizes (commits liability))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-D -- Customer draft over-apologizes (commits liability)
